SALES SCRIPT 





The following is the flow of how calls should be taken/handled. Try and follow this flow as closely as possible to ensure maximum success. 



First Call 



Establish rapport 

Frame the call 

Ask easy question 

Identify problem/issue 

Identify goals/objectives 

Get commitment/offer opportunity 

Establish next steps 

Send NDA 

Request ad account access 





Establish rapport 



Start off by bridging a genuine connection with the lead.  It’s easy - just be authentically interested in them as a human being, ask them questions like you would with anyone else you’ve just met.  If you can make them laugh - bonus points! 





Frame the call 



“Hey Samantha, ok so basically we’ve got about half an hour together and what I want to do is establish whether or not we can help you hit your business targets.  I’m going to ask you a few questions about your FB ad goals and activities and from that I’ll be able to see if it’s a good fit for both of us.  I won’t be trying to sell you anything or anything like that, it’s purely a discovery call. Sounds good?  Great, alright so why don’t you start by telling me a little bit more about your situation - how long have you been running FB ads for now?” 





Ask easy question 



“Great, alright so why don’t you start by telling me a little bit more about your situation - how long have you been running FB ads for now?” 



“Great, alright so why don’t you start by telling me a little bit more about your business?”



Identify problem/issue 



Here you want to be able to assess what exactly isn’t working - refer to checklist in SOP as well to ask correct tactical questions.  



Try and identify what the cost to them is by not achieving targets or goals or having poor performing ads - how is it affecting them on a personal level. 



Write down how they describe their problems, in their own words inside the CRM system. 



If you can slot in a mini case study or client comparison here even better! 





Identify goals/objectives 



“So in the past we signed NDAs with clients but we have a new rule now where we really only want to work with clients that will be able to give us testimonials down the track. What would need to happen in your business in the next six months for you to leave us a raving testimonial?”



Here you want to help them dreamscape - what outcomes are they desiring, what are the revenue and financial targets they’re aiming for? 



If you can slot in a mini case study or client comparison here even better! 



Write down how they describe their problems, in their own words inside the CRM system.







Get commitment/offer opportunity 



“Alright, so it sounds like we might be able to help you and I actually don’t see any reason why this wouldn’t work for you but here’s the situation - I’m not the one running the ads and the last thing we want to do is make you promises and onboard you and not be able to deliver as this would be a waste of time for both of us.”





If potential performance based:  “Alright, so it sounds like we might be able to help you and I actually don’t see any reason why this wouldn’t work for you but here’s the situation.  I know you’re interested in the performance based model, which we do run for clients, but this is a very risky model for us for obvious reasons so we need to make sure it’s going to be viable and worth out time.” 

If performance based but they clearly do NOT qualify: “Ok, so I know you’re interested in the performance based model however at this stage, based on what you’ve described, this wouldn’t be a model we could offer you simply as it would be too much risk on our end to take on.  If you wanted to discuss other ways to work together I’m more than happy to explore these with you but I can tell you - even without consulting the team - that for sure they would not approve your account under the performance based model.”





Establish next steps 



“So the next step is that I’ll need to run all of this past the team and get their take on it.  We would also need to check out what has happened in the ad account so what I can do now is send you through a signed NDA from us and set up access as an analyst (meaning we can’t touch anything) to the account to save us time.” 





Send NDA 



Follow SOP on this and confirm when this has been sent. 





Request ad account access 



Follow SOP on this and confirm when this has been sent. 





Wrap Up Call 



“Perfect!  Ok so please give us about 3-5 days to audit the account and come back with some recommendations.  In the meantime, please don’t hesitate to sing out if you have any questions!” 







Second Call 





Establish rapport 

Share audit findings 

Recap objectives/goals 

Recap problems/issues 

Explain solution 

Stack up 

Pitch 

Next steps 

Take deposit / set follow up reminder  









Establish Rapport 



Reconnect with the lead at this stage. Draw from last call to find common interest or conversational topic.  Bonus points if you can make them laugh. 





Recap Objectives/Goals 



Refer back to the notes you wrote down during first call on their goals and objectives and recap this for them:



“Ok, so last time we spoke you mentioned you’re trying to scale to XYZ and achieve 4 x ZXY.” 



Paraphrase them using the very same language they used on first call 





Recap Issues/Problems 



Refer back to the notes you wrote down during first call on their issues/problems and recap this for them:



“But you’re having issues with XYZ” eyc 



Paraphrase them using the very same language they used on first call 





Share audit findings 



Explain what the audit pulled up and findings from the audit. 



Try and share stories of clients or other campaigns that had similar issues that agency helped resolve 



If performance based but they do NOT qualify:  Start framing this to make them realise they don’t have anything setup or in place. 





Explain Solution 



Here is where you explain recommendations (without getting too DIY here - just very top level) 



If performance based but they do NOT qualify: “So unfortunately after reviewing the account we simply can’t accept you as a client under the performance based model as this would simply be too risky for us at this stage.”  Usually the lead will prompt the question of their options, and here you can offer them opportunity to work with agency following normal flow. 





Stack Up 



“Our goal here is to become your marketing partner - because the more we grow your business, the more we grow ours as well, so we’re really excited! Alright, so you’re looking to make XYZ in your business over the next six months - a growth of XYZ from where you currently are.  After reviewing everything, and based on previous clients that were in a very similar situation, we’re very confident we can make this happen for you.”	





Pitch 



“The cost to set everything up would be just XYZ and we would require a three month commitment at XYZ.  So how can we make this work for you” 



And then DON’T SPEAK!  Don’t utter a word or else you will disrupt the buyer consideration phase.  Wait for them to prompt next question and address these as they come up. 





Next Steps 



Awesome!  Ok so here’s what’s going to happen.  I’m going to send through a contract and briefing document, the sooner you can fill this in the sooner we can start scoping out your funnel and campaigns. I’ll also introduce you to XYZ who will be responsible for making sure you’re looked after! 





Deposit 



“So we require an $XYZ deposit in order to secure you space in our books. Would you like to place that on VISA or another form of card? 





COMMON OBJECTIONS 





And How to Handle Them 





I thought this was performance-based…



As discussed we do have a performance based model in the business, however to qualify you need to have a validated funnel. At this stage [explain their situation]



...you’re essentially asking me to get passenger for your bus, which I’m excited about, but the thing is you don’t even have the bus yet.  So regardless if you choose to work with me or someone else - you’re going to need that bus to make a real go of this. 





Well, what’s the point of performance based if it only works for those who already have good campaigns? 



A number of reasons really.  A lot of the times, the funnels work, but are not optimised properly or the client will have a hard time to scale the campaigns.  Sometimes the ads were created by the owner, who - as they grew and became more successful- realised it wasn’t worth their time anymore to manage. 





It’s too expensive…



I understand. Many of my clients felt that same way when they first started working with me and now they’re spending about twice as much without even batting an eyelash. Here’s the honest truth, $10K is nice but that’s not where I make my money from you. I make my money from you when we work together for the next three years and I’m able to help scale your business - THAT’s where the real money is for me. My evil plan if you will is to make it so that you make so much money $10K never seems like a lot of money for you again!







What’s the guarantee?



I don’t own Facebook so it would be foolish of me to give you a guarantee, and anyone who does it full of BS for this very reason!  



My guarantee is that if you DON’T work with me you’ll be in the same place in six months and one year from now. 







Do you have testimonials or case studies I can have a look at? 



All the work I conduct with clients is under NDAs so I can’t show you any ad accounts but feel free to do your due diligence. Our team is one of the top teams in the world. 





Can I speak to one of your clients?



Look, I hope you can appreciate that we speak to hundreds of people per month. If we were to hand out our client numbers this would become a big nuisance for them and my number one priority is respecting the relationship I have with them. 



I promise you - however - that when we start working together I will uphold the same policy for you as well, how does that sound? 







Let me think about it… 



We offer the discounted price to you as we don’t like to chase up clients that are unsure. This saves us time and saves you money.  I can save the discount at this price until (48 hours later) - after which it would be the full price.  



OR



Sure thing. We have a couple of projects coming up on our end so would you like to set up some time in (X days) to make a call and answer any other questions you might have?
